name: agent-test
description: Use for AGENT TEST — designing the measurement an AI agent or skill is judged by. DO — write evals, judges, trajectory tests, benchmarks, activation evals. REVIEW — audit eval suite trustworthiness. DESIGN — shape failure-mode-first eval suites. Triggers: 'design our agent evals', 'is our LLM judge trustworthy', 'are trajectory tests real'

inspired_by.name: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
inspired_by.author: Lianmin Zheng
inspired_by.contribution: Documents LLM-judge biases (position, verbosity/length, self-preference) and the need to calibrate a judge against human preference before trusting it — the basis for the judge-calibration discipline and the Vague Judge anti-pattern.

inspired_by.name: Improving Ratings: Audit in the British University System (Goodhart's Law)
inspired_by.author: Marilyn Strathern
inspired_by.contribution: When a measure becomes a target it ceases to be a good measure — the metric-gaming risk that benchmark and eval design must resist by rewarding behavior, not a proxy.

metadata.catalog_summary: Agent-as-subject-under-measurement: design the measurement an AI agent or skill is judged by — DO (write the smallest gating eval/judge/test), REVIEW (audit an eval suite, judge calibration, trajectory tests, benchmarks, or activation evals for trustworthiness, then score it), DESIGN (shape a failure-mode-first suite, calibrate a judge, build a held-out benchmark, design activation evals).
